nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 雉科
nodes.summary: 雉科（Phasianidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联179种鸟类，如Udzungwa forest partridge、銹紅林鷓鴣、冠鷓鴣等179种鸟类。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸡形目
nodes.summary: 鸡形目（Galliformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联298种鸟类，如眼斑冢雉、Red-billed brushturkey、Black-billed brushturkey等298种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沙鹑属
nodes.summary: 沙鹑属（Ammoperdix）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联2种鸟类，如See-see partridge、Sand partridge。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: See-see partridge
nodes.summary: See-see partridge（Ammoperdix griseogularis）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如See-see partridge。

nodes.name: See-see partridge
nodes.summary: The see-see partridge (Ammoperdix griseogularis) is a gamebird in the pheasant family Phasianidae of the order Galliformes, gallinaceous birds. This partridge has its main native range from southeast Turkey through Syria and Iraq east to Iran and Pakistan. It is closely related and similar to its counterpart in Egypt and Arabia, the sand partridge, Ammoperdix heyi. This 22–25 cm bird is a resident breeder in dry, open and often hilly country. It nests in a scantily lined ground scrape laying 8-16 eggs. The see-see partridge takes a wide variety of seeds and some insect food. See-see partridge is a rotund bird, mainly sandy-brown with wavy white and brown flank stripes. The male has a grey head with a black stripe through the eye and a white cheek patch. The neck sides are speckled with white. The head pattern is the best distinction from sand partridge. The female is a very washed-out version of the male, and is more difficult to distinguish from its relative due to the weak head pattern. Usually it is seen in pairs or, at the most, in flocks of two to four birds. But flocks of more than fifty birds have also been seen. When disturbed, see-see partridge prefers to run rather than fly, but if necessary it flies a short distance on rounded wings. The song is a whistled hwit-hwit-hwit. References Pheasants, Partridges and Grouse by Madge and McGowan, ISBN 0-7136-3966-0
